name: Chile Rellenos (Stuffed Chiles)
recipeIngredient: Lard or reserved fat from the meat, 6 tbsp
Acitron or candied fruit, chopped, 2 tbsp
Lard or reserved fat from the tomatoes, 1/4 cup
recipeInstructions:
1. Prepare the picadillo: Cut pork into large cubes and cook with onion, garlic, and salt in cold water until tender (40-45 minutes). Strain and shred or chop the meat. Set aside to cool completely; reserve broth.
2. Melt lard in a pan and cook onion and garlic without browning. Add meat and cook until browned. Crush spices and add them with other ingredients. Cook mixture briefly, then mash tomatoes and stir into meat.
3. Prepare tomato broth: Blend tomatoes, onions, and garlic until smooth; fry the puree in lard for 3 minutes. Add remaining ingredients and cook for 5 minutes over high heat. Add reserved pork broth and simmer for 15 minutes to reduce slightly. Season with salt as needed.
4. Make batter: Whisk egg whites until foamy, then mix in yolks, salt, and flour until smooth.
5. Blanch chiles in boiling water for 30 seconds; drain. Place the meat filling into each chile and coat generously with batter. Fry slowly in deep fat until golden brown (about 10 minutes).
6. Serve hot in a bowl of tomato broth.
